seo.title: About — Auth Fly | Open auth construction kit
seo.description: Auth Fly is an open auth construction kit: a self-hosted SAML 2.0 + OIDC IdP, a UI8Kit-based hosted UI, and adaptive SDKs. Mission, working principles, technical references and roadmap from PoC to MVP.
seo.keywords: Auth Fly about, open source SSO, self-hosted IdP, AuthKit, UI8Kit, AuthKit TS, SSO SPI, security in the open, no telemetry

books.title: Specs & checklists
books.intro: Practical references for teams running their own IdP — protocols, hosted UI, and a public security checklist.

bio.short: Auth Fly is an open construction kit for authentication: a self-hosted SAML 2.0 + OIDC Identity Provider, a hosted UI on UI8Kit, and adaptive SDKs that fit any language or framework. PoC is live with Hanko as the credential backend; MVP runs on Supabase or your own Postgres through the SSO SPI.
long: The through-line is boring reliability: provider-agnostic core contracts, narrow adapters per credential backend, observability over storytelling, and documentation that matches production behaviour.
Security is not a feature — it is the spine of the project. Hardening is tracked publicly via a pre-flight checklist, with no telemetry and no third-party update channels in the critical path.

values.title: Own your IdP
values.desc: Self-hosted, no telemetry, no third-party update calls. The IdP runs on your origin and only on your origin.

values.title: Adaptive SDKs
values.desc: One TypeScript core for the browser; thin adapters per provider and per language. New stacks ship as adapters, not rewrites.

values.title: Provider-agnostic core
values.desc: Core contracts depend on no UI and no backend. Swap Hanko for Supabase or a custom Postgres without touching the IdP or the UI.

values.title: Security in the open
values.desc: Hardening tracked publicly with a pre-flight checklist. Strong points and gaps are listed side by side.

books.title: Auth Fly IdP — README
books.desc: SAML 2.0 + OIDC dual-protocol IdP: endpoints, configuration, environment overrides, and cross-protocol SSO behaviour.

books.title: AuthKit & UI8Kit notes
books.desc: Hosted UI on Go + templ: Renderer, ViewConfig, FlowConfig, embedded i18n, and the migration from Hanko Elements to UI8Kit.

books.title: Pre-flight security checklist
books.desc: Public hardening list grouped into four stages: critical fixes, protocol hardening, web security, and enterprise features.

timeline.label: Architecture
timeline.detail: core / authkit / authkit-hanko split, contracts before code

timeline.label: PoC live
timeline.detail: SAML 2.0 + OIDC sharing one signed IdP session, Hanko as credential backend

timeline.label: UI migration
timeline.detail: Hanko Elements replaced one-to-one by UI8Kit across hosted login UI

timeline.label: Stage 1 hardening
timeline.detail: Critical fixes: HTTP timeouts, JWT iss/aud/alg, session_key validation, key file mode 0600

timeline.label: Stage 2 protocol hardening
timeline.detail: PKCE, at_hash, OIDC iss check, SAML IssueInstant / Destination / anti-replay

timeline.label: Stage 3 web security
timeline.detail: Security headers, CSRF on admin, rate limiting, MaxBytesReader, graceful shutdown

timeline.label: Stage 4 enterprise
timeline.detail: Key rotation with multi-key JWKS, RSA-4096, SAML SLO, structured audit logs

timeline.label: SSO SPI expansion
timeline.detail: Supabase and custom Postgres backends behind the same CredentialVerifier contract

timeline.label: Adaptive SDKs
timeline.detail: AuthKit TS core plus thin SDKs for Go, Node, Python, PHP and .NET
